video_title: Top #DDD Videos on the West Coast with Guy Fieri | Food Network

restaurants.name: Jitlada
restaurants.guy_intro: The best Thai food in the country. We have the spicy mint noodles with shrimp. There's regular Thai food, and there's gelada. What is it about this place that brings you back? Jazz. Jazz. I think that's the thing right there. Jazz is known for bringing the heat. Something Jazz Singsanong definitely showed us on Triple G. And she's bringing it here every day. After her brother, Chef Thuy Sung-gumi, took over this joint back in 06. I'm a customer of Jit Ladak. You were a customer? Yeah. Before your brother owned it? Yeah. 1969. It's been here that long? 1969. Seriously? This used to be a northern Thai restaurant. And then they bought it. And it became the best southern Thai restaurant in America.

dishes.description: Green curry paste made with serrano chili, Thai chili, garlic, red onion, lemongrass, Sri Lanka lime skin and leaves, cilantro. Cooked in a wok with coconut milk, fish sauce, eggplant, bamboo shoots, bell pepper, and Thai basil.
dishes.guy_response: Dude, wow. Oh, my gosh. One for you guys. It is going to be so hot. Oh, dude, that's hot. Oh, my god. I just ate the enamel off my teeth. That's delicious. But there's so much flavor. It's not just spicy. Do we have rice or anything? Yes. You pour it over the top? Yeah, so here. Oh, I want that eggplant. The color's incredible. This is what I want. This is what I really appreciate about this. It is hot, but it's not unpleasantly hot where it's destroying your taste buds. It's just this warm heat throughout your face. Yeah. It's so alive. We're just hitting every taste bud. The problem is, is I don't want to stop. I know. I know. Your crave-ability is through the roof. So good, Jaz. Green curry chicken. It's phenomenal. The spices are nice and complex. Once you try it, you really can't put that spoon down.

restaurants.name: Brendan and Rachel's Wood-Fired Neapolitan Pizza Joint
restaurants.guy_intro: And for husband-wife owners, Brendan Smith and Rachel Greenspan, their wood-fired Neapolitan pizza joint, all starts with the dough. Before I got into pizza, I was really a bread baker. Neither of us is Italian. Jewish and Irish.

dishes.description: Risotto balls with cacio e pepe sauce and fresh mozzarella, breaded with oregano and black pepper, frozen, then deep-fried. Served with Calabrian chili aioli.
dishes.guy_response: There's a lot of things going on here. When you can fry it to where it tastes baked, not oily, you have a nice, light, tender, flaky, fall apart. You look at it the wrong way, it'll just crumble. You've got good sweet. And you've got great texture. And you got good salt. But it's the spice in the Calabrian chili oil that sings all the way through, mirrored with the smokiness of that paprika. Poet with a palate. Right here, brother. That's it. We are done. Arancini coming out. The outside is just crispy. It melts in your mouth. It has the peppery, cheesy flavor.

restaurants.name: The Fuego
restaurants.guy_intro: Because John Atkinson has been at it for more than 25 years. But eight years ago, he hit a wall. I quit the restaurant business for a while, jumped on a plane to Rome, one-way ticket. No. And that was it, Italy. And that's how it started. That's how it started. When he came back, he opened his own joint with his new favorite recipe. Everything is fresh. They do it right.

dishes.dish_name: Kahlua Pig Monte Cristo Egg Rolls
dishes.description: Kahlua pig (pork butt, smoked salt, banana leaf, water, slow-cooked for 12 hours). Egg roll assembled with Swiss cheese, black forest ham, Kahlua pig, scallion, teriyaki, sesame seed. Fried. Served with wasabi mojo and teriyaki sauce.
dishes.guy_response: The Kahlua pig just really rocks and rolls. Mm. Green onion, ham, cheese, and a little bit of the wasabi mojo. I just want to hear you say money. That's like bank. Bank. I mean, there's money, and then there's bank. Kahlua pork egg roll. The sauce complements the pork. It melts in your mouth. Delicious. This place is awesome.

restaurants.name: Shalhoub Meat Company
restaurants.guy_intro: I mean, who knows meat better than a butcher? And here, John and LJ Shalhoub are second and third generation. Your dad started this? Yeah, the meat company. We converted it from just meat. Just the meat company to the more restaurant side of things. And that's when Chef Cosmo joined the team.

dishes.description: Chicken thighs dry-brined and grilled. Mole sauce made with puya, guajillo, and ancho chilies, spices, tomatoes, onions, jalapenos, cilantro, garlic, almonds, and chicken stock. Served in homemade corn tortillas with pickled onions, cilantro, and queso fresco.
dishes.guy_response: Mole is dynamite. It reminds me of Christmas spice. That's a completely different mole than I've had. It's spicy, but not hot spicy. You know you're eating something different. But I'll tell you what the thing is, chef, that I really get the biggest kick out of is this corn flour tortilla. I want a quesadilla in that tortilla. Delicious. Dynamite mole, I mean, the whole thing. Well done, my friend.

restaurants.name: Nadia's Thai-Inspired Burrito Joint
restaurants.guy_intro: That's because when Nadia Ajlooni took over this joint, she was looking to make everyone happy, including herself. Combining all of my interests, hospitality, cooking, business, music, bringing people together, this seemed like the perfect job. Thank you guys for coming in. I'm here during lunch, late night. It's a great place to hang out, great food.

dishes.description: Tri-tip marinated in oyster sauce, crushed garlic, black pepper, garlic salt. Nom tok sauce made with fish sauce, soy sauce, lime juice, shallots, Thai basil, mint, lime leaves, sambal, red chili flake, sugar, and rice powder. Basil mayo made with mayonnaise, garlic oil, Thai basil, pepper, and fresh garlic. Assembled on a tortilla with rice, beef, green onions, cilantro, spring mix, nom tok sauce, and basil mayo, then pressed in a panini press.
dishes.guy_response: Beautiful. I love the toasted tortilla. The fresh herbs inside of the nom tok, also inside of this veggie mix with the baby greens, that herbaceousness, the fresh crunch and flavor of that sings in a different way than anything that's cooked. You get this tender, but seasoned beef. Those are kind of like the little surprises. Those little nuggets. I've been to three restaurants today. I'm eating that burrito like it's the first thing I've had all day. Oh, damn it. The nom tok burrito is spice of life, hands down.

restaurants.name: Belly & Snout
restaurants.guy_intro: We're in Koreatown, but this is Filipino. Yes. Filipino-American. Which means since 2014, owner Warren Almeida has been mashing up American faves with traditional Filipino flavors. Chef Warren, you know what he's doing? He's doing the Filipino people proud.

dishes.description: Sisig (pork snout, pork belly, salt, garlic, water, simmered for 3 hours, then ground with bell peppers, jalapenos, red onions, cooked on flat top). Served on a beef hot dog with garlic crema, pickled red onions, sliced red radish, green onions, calamansi, and chicharron.
dishes.guy_response: The snout's what it's all about. It eats bigger than probably any hot dog I've ever had. The dog itself is really good. The pickled onions play in the garlic sour cream. Definitely adds some flavor, a little acid. But that sisig right on top, it's crunchy, it's tender, it's unctuous, it's fatty. It's just a really nice complement to it. I would want a whole sandwich of that. It has so many layers. Every bite, you get a really good combination of stuff.

restaurants.name: Bludsoe's BBQ
restaurants.guy_intro: That's California native and pit master Kevin Bludsoe, who learned everything he knows about barbecue from his Texan grandmother. Granny had a little small shack in Corsicana, Texas. Every summer, my mother and father would send me down there. Smoked ham hocks. Granny's gravy on there. What year was it? 2013? 13, baby, yeah. Did you think this is where it was going to end up? You never know. Do your barbecue. Just don't do no bad. Don't do no bad barbecue.

dishes.description: Pork ribs dry-rubbed with brown sugar, white sugar, seasoning salt, chili powder, granulated garlic, coarse black pepper, cumin, cayenne pepper, oregano, and granulated onion. Marinated for 24 hours, then smoked at 250 degrees for 3.5-4 hours.
dishes.guy_response: I'll tell you what, this is a righteous rib. All that flavor just coming out of that bone, onto that pork. Man. All this is your grandma's recipe? All of it.

dishes.description: Rib tips seasoned with garlic salt and coarse black pepper. Smoked at 250 degrees with post oak and pecan wood for 2 hours. Cut up, fried, then sauced with homemade sauce (hot sauce, barbecue sauce, garlic, and other ingredients).
dishes.guy_response: Here's the thing I love about them. I love my wings really well done. I don't want them to be fatty and chewy and gooey. I want them to have some crisp to them. That's what's happening here. You've smoked them. The flavor's going all the way through. And then you're rehydrating them with this buffalo sauce. So you get all the texture, all the smoke, all the flavor. They're fun to eat. That's delicious.

restaurants.name: Wilma & Frida's
restaurants.guy_intro: And it was that enthusiasm that convinced Kelly McFall and her brother Craig Alexander to open their second location. We knew we wanted a bigger place, and we fell in love with this location. It's right in the heart of Palm Springs. So what is the restaurant? What do you guys do? It's breakfast and lunch. Wilman Frida is our two grandmothers. Are your grandmas still with you? No. No, they passed away. Did they know about the naming? Wilman did. That's why it's Wilman Frida. Yes. We had to put Wilman first. Scratch made? 99% of everything is done from scratch here.

dishes.description: Boneless short rib braised overnight at 250 degrees with black peppercorn, garlic clove, bay leaves, ground thyme, kosher salt, burgundy red wine, mirepoix, and beef base. Served on sourdough bread with Monterey Jack cheese, pickled red onions, and sauteed arugula.
dishes.guy_response: Short rib's tender. Arugula's a really nice touch. This guy's got it. I got French rice with the short rib melt. Our number one seller hands down of anything.

dishes.description: Marinara sauce, ricotta mix (ricotta, feta, cream cheese, sour cream, basil, oregano, salt, pepper, garlic), and ragu (ground beef, diced onion, Italian seasoning, garlic, red wine, marinara). Layered with sliced sweet potatoes and baked. Topped with extra marinara and party cheese.
dishes.guy_response: Your sweet potato holds together. You got a hearty marinara. That's all of the lasagna you want with none of the garlic. The guilt of the carb of the pasta. Out of the park, buddy. Well done.

restaurants.name: Kurt's Smokehouse
restaurants.guy_intro: All from the back of a souped up rig towed by a real deal chef turned pit master, Kurt Aldridge. You're bringing in old school low and slow barbecue on the stick burner. Yes. That is the least easy thing in the world. I serve two days a week. It takes me the rest of the week to get ready. You're an animal.

dishes.description: Pulled pork (pork butt dry-rubbed, air-dried, smoked with almond wood, wrapped in Texas peach paper, smoked again). Mojo Criollo (red onion, garlic, orange juice, lemon juice, lime juice, oregano, salt, oil). Sour sausage (pork, fermento, dextrose, cayenne pepper, Korean red pepper, spices, smoked). Bacon (dry-brined, smoked). House-made barbecue sauce. Served on a house-made bun with pickled veggies and sriracha mayo.
dishes.guy_response: That's all you need to serve, bro. That gives me goosebumps. Bacon's delicious. Rendered enough. When you're working with such few ingredients and such simple equipment, your discipline and your expertise have to be spot on, which they are. This is high-level barbecue.

restaurants.name: The Shawarma Guys
restaurants.guy_intro: And has been ever since Brian Zito canceled his cell phone career plan to become a shawarma man. Who taught you how to make shawarma? One of the first times I saw shawarma being made was on Guy's Big Bite. That gives me goosebumps, dude. And now he's rolling out like a pro. What separates it from the other food trucks is the flavor of the food, the mix of spices.

dishes.description: Chicken shawarma (leg and breast, marinated in Greek yogurt, lemon juice, spices, and herbs). Fattoush salad dressing (garlic paste, sumac, mint, salt, red wine vinegar, lemon juice, oil). Fattoush salad (romaine, cucumbers, tomatoes, red onions, radishes, green peppers, fresh mint, sumac, fresh pita chips). Assembled on a tortilla with lettuce, chicken shawarma, and garlic paste, then pressed in a panini press.
dishes.guy_response: Love the radishes in there. The little added extra crunch of the bell peppers, the cucumbers. Delicious. Chicken shawarma going on the fattoush wrap. The chicken is so tender. It's like the perfect blend of juicy and the crunch. I love it. Definitely one of my favorites. When people say to me they want to have a food truck and they want to have a restaurant, they want to get into the business, it's kind of like 90% want it, 10% can make it happen. You're one of those people that has the discipline, has a palate, has the drive, has the inspiration to make it work. And dude, you're making it work and then some. You're looking for the real deal? It's right here, shawarma guys.

restaurants.name: Tioli's Crazy Burger
restaurants.owner_chef: Lothar Manns and Wolfgang Peter Schlick
restaurants.guy_intro: When you think about a burger joint, well, you're probably thinking the all-American burger with the shake and the fries and the whole giddy-up, unless you're here in San Diego, where two dudes from Germany who got out of fine dining, well, these cats will make a burger out of just about anything. You got to check out Tioli's Crazy Burger.

dishes.description: Ground alligator sirloin, seasoned with salt, pepper, paprika, and garlic powder. Served with a curry fruit gravy made from dried bananas, apples, apricots, plums, curry, and cream.
dishes.guy_response: That's great. I gotta tell you, I never thought I would really like it. Look at that. That's awesome. Great flavor to it. And the curry fruit thing, you know what? That's crazy. Actually tastes like a pork cutlet. It seems like all the flavors meld together to make one very fine burger.

dishes.description: Lean kangaroo meat (90-10), seasoned with salt, pepper, paprika, and garlic powder. Served with horseradish cream (mayonnaise, sour cream, horseradish, salt, white pepper, lemon juice).
dishes.guy_response: Great flavor, not overcooked. Horseradish cream sauce is dynamite on there. I like that. And so do you. Huh? Of course I like what I cook. Oh, sorry. It's getting out of control. It's very lean. It's very good. It's very unique. If I didn't know it was something different, I would think it's hamburger.

dishes.description: A half-pound lamb and half-pound beef patty, seasoned with salt and pepper. Served with tzatziki sauce (sour cream, yogurt, cucumber, fresh garlic, fresh mint, salt, white pepper, lemon juice).
dishes.guy_response: Look how juicy that is. That's awesome. The tzatziki and the lamb and the beef, man, that's good. And the sauce, it's amazing. The lamb and sauce all together makes a really great burger.

restaurants.name: Marouch
restaurants.guy_intro: We live down the street. We pay the sunshine tax and the Marouche fee, and it's so worth it. I've had a lot of Lebanese-Armenian food. This is easily the best. And it stayed that way even after Haikuye Tatarian took over from the original owners in 2018. And she brought in her mom, Nellie, to run the kitchen.

dishes.description: Tri-tip marinated for two days in vinegar, white wine, oil, Old Spice, Seven Spice, black pepper, cardamom, salt, onions, bay leaves, and lemon juice. Cooked on a rotisserie, topped with fat and onion. Served with hummus and pilaf.
dishes.guy_response: It's really light and fluffy. I feel shawarma from the shawarma. The flavor of the lemon is incredible, and how it sort of tenderizes the meat. There's so much flavor in the meat. And when you get that hummus with it and the pilaf, that's a meal right there. Beautiful. This is the way I want to eat five nights a week. Delicious.

restaurants.name: Dongbei Kexin Chinese Food
restaurants.guy_intro: And they're Wendy Lee's mom's recipes, which she started serving when she took over this more than 70-year-old Portland icon. This is Northeastern Chinese food from my hometown, Dongbei Kexin. What is the most popular Chinese food from Dongbei? The dumplings. The dumplings I would call plumplings because they're so plump. Just delicious.

dishes.description: Handmade dough (high-gluten flour, water, salt, soybean oil). Filling made with pork butt, water, soy sauce, oyster sauce, dark soy sauce, ginger, green onion, chicken base, salt, house oil with Sichuan peppercorn, soybean oil, leeks, and shrimp. Boiled for 6 minutes. Served with garlic and soy sauce for dipping.
dishes.guy_response: Oh. Are you OK? You understand this is like my kryptonite. It's so good. Juicy. You get the tenderness of the shrimp. Still get a little crunch of the leek. The noodle that wraps the dumpling is the key. It's that pillowy, soft, but not too thick dough, combined with all of the fantastic ingredients inside of it and this delicious dipping sauce. Outstanding.

restaurants.name: Farouk and Salim's Burger Joint
restaurants.guy_intro: We're going to make the Buddha burger. It's a turkey burger. It has different textures to it and different spices. Cumin, curry. It's delicious. So we're starting off with ground turkey. Yeah. Salt and pepper, curry powder, some cumin. This has a little bit of an Indian flair, I'm taking it. Kind of zen. Kind of zen? Yeah, it'll make you feel good.

dishes.description: Ground turkey seasoned with salt, pepper, curry powder, cumin, soy sauce, chopped garlic, fresh ginger, garlic chili sauce, chopped red peppers, cilantro, and green onion. Served on a toasted sesame seed bun with sweet chili mayo, Napa cabbage, tomato, bean sprouts, fresh red pepper, and cilantro sprigs.
dishes.guy_response: I get burned out on just the same burger. This right here is a complete departure from it. It's built like a burger, but that's about the end of it. The rest of it is turning into this Mediterranean flavor based, not overdone. Like if someone says, well, I don't like curry. It's just too much. You just got a nice hint of it. The Napa cabbage and the bean sprout make everything come together. Great burger, not dried out. Turkey's easy to dry out. Yeah. This is a delicious spot on burger. Well done.

restaurants.name: Catherine's Mom's Chilaquiles Joint
restaurants.guy_intro: And the chef doing the spinning here is Catherine Gonzalez, who didn't have to go far from home to find her inspiration. I'm using recipes from mom, and then I kind of infused it with some of the things I learned. Mom's red chili chilaquiles. The chilaquiles are fantastic. I could eat those every day. The meat is so tender. The sauce is complex and flavorful.

dishes.description: Pork cushion dry-rubbed and braised. Red chile sauce made from rehydrated California chile, Mexican oregano, cumin, onion, garlic, and salt. Date and onion jam. Assembled with house-made corn chips, red chile sauce, pulled pork, green onion, queso fresco, avocado, microgreens, chili oil, and onion jam.
dishes.guy_response: The chile sauce, it's silky, a lot of chile flavor. You do this really dynamite pork. You go a little bit extra with that. The chips are nice and crunchy. The jam is just that kind of that funky little twist that makes it yours. That's the next level.

restaurants.name: Chef Antonia's Eclectic Joint
restaurants.guy_intro: And it should, because Chef Antonia LaFaso learned from a lot of the greats in New York and LA before she set out on her own. I had this moment where I was like, I want to do food that represents me. And your way of doing things is know everything about it, compose it to a plate, and live it. I feel like you're my avatar. It's like, you see me. Seriously. Let's plug our hair in.

dishes.description: Sliced hamachi. Yuzu ponzu (jalapenos, Thai basil, yuzu, fresh orange juice, tamari, sesame oil, steeped for two days). Tempura broccolini (broccolini coated in all-purpose flour, rice flour, cornstarch, kosher salt, baking powder, water, then fried). Shiso aioli. Assembled with yuzu ponzu, hamachi, Thai basil, mint, shiso aioli, and tempura broccolini. Garnished with black sesame and green onion.
dishes.guy_response: You hit all the marks here. Fresh, eclectic. You're giving crunchy, you're giving supple, you're giving raw. Spicy factor, sweet factor. I mean, just check, check, check, check, check, check, check the box. It is delicate and balanced. That would compete with any Japanese food. Any Japanese restaurant on any level. Thank you.

restaurants.name: Harold & Belle's
restaurants.guy_intro: This restaurant has been in the same family for 47 years. Going back to 1969, when Louisiana Transplants, Harold and Marybelle Legault opened a dive bar serving up gumbo and po' boys. Now five decades and two renovations later, grandson Ryan's still cooking up the Creole. All four of my grandparents are from New Orleans. I have a very Creole last name, Legault, L-E-G-A-U-X. The only people that have X in their name are people that come from Louisiana. I don't find much Cajun Creole food in the West Coast. So when I came here and I met this guy and I tried the food, I'm like, you're the real deal, brother.

dishes.description: Seafood stock, trinity (peppers, celery, onions), filet gumbo (ground sassafras leaf), Creole seasoning, spices, and roux. Contains boneless skinless chicken thighs, beef hot links, diced pitham, smoked sausage, blue crab, and shrimp.
dishes.guy_response: That's fantastic. You pick up that seafood, even though there was just a little bit of that blue crab. You get, mm, velvety texture coming over there. It's coming over your tongue from that sassafras and getting it from that roux. Oh, I just sit here and eat it all day.

dishes.description: Roux (canola oil, olive oil, flour). Trinity (red and green bell peppers, onions, celery). Creole seasoning, spices, hot sauce, Worcestershire. Tomato soup, shrimp stock. Crawfish and cream of mushroom soup.
dishes.guy_response: I mean, this is the first time I had real etouffee. And there's so much flavor going on in this. All the spices. I mean, you're just talking deep and rich and creamy. And you get nice little tender crawfish inside of it. Crawfish is an intensified shrimp flavor and a great meaty flavor. It's got a meaty texture, kind of like lobster. Mm. You'll love this.

restaurants.name: Rangel Family Burgers
restaurants.guy_intro: That's because here, Greg and Belinda Rangel, along with their son, Ruben Reyes, are serving patties their way. Unique combinations. Kind of more gourmet, juiciest burger you can have. Well, you got me a hello on all that. Sounds good. They grind all their own meat. The flavor, the texture, it's just really, really delicious.

dishes.description: Burger patty served on a potato bun with brie cheese, frisee lettuce, lobster, and crispy onions. Features lemon aioli, romesco sauce, and garlic anchovy aioli.
dishes.guy_response: There's so much big flavor in this. With the romesco, the brie, the anchovy aioli, then on top of it with the lobster, a very sophisticated burger. Your flavors all coincide together. They work together very well. They add texture to it, too. That's the real deal right there. That's the one. Great job, chef.

restaurants.name: Señor Sisig
restaurants.guy_intro: Back in 2010, they started mixing Mexican with, get this, a sour pork Filipino dish called sisig. Filipino food is something that people are trying to get acquainted to. Not many people know about it, and Senior Sisig is doing a great job introducing it to the general public.

dishes.description: Chicken drumettes and wings marinated for 5 hours in soy sauce, sugarcane vinegar, garlic, bay leaves, crushed peppercorn, and salt. Hard-baked halfway, then deep-fried. Double-glazed with adobo glaze (olive oil, garlic, peppercorn, bay leaves, soy sauce, sugarcane vinegar, chipotle peppers, ketchup, honey, brown sugar, cayenne pepper, red chili flakes, Worcestershire sauce, salt, corn starch, water). Served on shrimp chips, garnished with green onion lemon zest.
dishes.guy_response: I can't figure out the flavor. All of a sudden, I got soy sauce. Now I get chipotle. Now I get a little bit of the garlic. Now I get sweet. Yeah. The wings are cooked perfectly. Little big wings. Great crust to them. And the shrimp chips has accompanied me through the whole process. All right. All right. All right. All right. All right. All right. All right. All right. Those shrimp chips as accompaniment. Right on. For sure. Not too sweet. Not too spicy. Not Asian. Not Mexican. Just senior seasick. Yeah. Nice to do. Right on, my friend.

restaurants.name: Felipe and Lizbeth's Mexican Kitchen
restaurants.guy_intro: The food here is very similar to my hometown. Probably because Felipe Castaneda and his wife, Lizbeth, have been serving up authentic recipes from their hometowns for decades. What type of Mexican food is this? Zacatecas, Jalisco region. Okay. North central. Our food is not too heavy in spices.

dishes.description: Chicken breast (bone on, skin on) cooked in water with garlic, tomatoes, onion, and salt. Pipián sauce made with toasted and soaked guajillo, chile de árbol, and California chiles, pumpkin seeds with shell, blue corn, chicken broth, and water. Served with Mexican rice, salsa de una diabla, and tortillas.
dishes.guy_response: The freshness of the tortilla is really good flavor. The chicken is tender. The diabla. Hello. The sauce is hot. And the pipián, it's got the flavor of the enchilada, but the richness of the mole. Outstanding. The rice is nice and light and flaky. The beans are creamy and silky. That's gotta be something you could serve to your mom. I mean, legit.

restaurants.name: The Church of Pizza
restaurants.guy_intro: It certainly was a heavenly sight for Chad and Cinnamon Nemec when they spotted this place for sale while visiting from Austin. We went home from our vacation. Two weeks later, came and put a contract in. Was it intended to be a house, or was it intended to be a pizza joint? I wanted a house. He wanted a pizzeria. It really, really is beautiful. The building was built in 1912. It's on the National Historic Registry. Who's the pastor of pizza? Adam. Where'd you learn to cook? Just had a passion for craft things.

dishes.description: Pulled pork (pork butt scored, gochujang sauce, salt, pepper, bound, smoked with almond wood, shredded). Dough (hard red wheat flour, yeast, water, salt, double-proofed). Colby sauce (black pepper, brown sugar, garlic, green onion, ginger, grated yellow onion, sesame oil, mirin, soy sauce). Pineapple slaw (ginger, shallots, chopped cilantro, pineapple, serranos). Assembled with Colby sauce, fresh mozzarella, gochujang pork, fontina cheese. Wood-fired, then topped with pineapple slaw, pickled onion, and garlic.
dishes.guy_response: And dynamite. That crust is so light and airy and fluffy. The balance that you create with that pineapple slaw, because you're getting a lot of heat from the serranos and the jalapenos, with the acidity from the onions countering the brown sugar that you have in the colby sauce, that's what gives it the balance and the harmony. I know I'm preaching to the choir on this, but I got to confess, it's excellent pizza.

restaurants.name: Beer Belly
restaurants.guy_intro: Koreatown obviously has a lot of different fusion places, not German and Korean. But that didn't stop owner Neil Kwan from playing matchmaker. What gave you the inspiration to do a Korean fusion, funky craft beer joint? After college, we backpacked to Europe. One of the stops, Germany. If anything, I wanted to capture that vibe. With the help of Chef Eddie Ha, who started mixing things up in the kitchen in 2011.

dishes.description: Chicken brined for 24 hours in Korean beer (Hype), water, honey, parsley, lemon, garlic, bay leaf, dry oregano, kosher salt, and black pepper. Pat dry, seasoned with salt, pepper, and oil. Cooked in oven at 400 degrees for 35 minutes on a Guinness beer can. Served with cabbage frisee salad, pickled jalapeno, and pickled daikon.
dishes.guy_response: Oh. We like to do beer can chicken, but I don't think I've ever had one that doesn't have this much flavor of beer. You get some of that nice roasted flavor from the skin. Then you get a little bite of that slaw. We'll buy that jalapeno. Chef, nicely done. Thank you, Chef. The skin's crispy. The meat's juicy. I get it every time I come here.

restaurants.name: Jeff and Blake's Craft Beer & Waffle Bar
restaurants.guy_intro: Beer is often the draw, but food will keep you here. Which is exactly how owners Jeff Brown and Blake Humble drew it up. It kind of started as a craft beer bar, but we wanted people to leave saying, damn, that food's good. So they signed up Chef Brian Lane. Another hometown dude who turned Jeff and Blake's dream menu into a reality with dishes like this trifecta.

dishes.description: Waffle made with all-purpose flour, granulated sugar, baking powder, kosher salt, eggs, buttermilk, and vanilla. Ham cooked in maple mustard butter. Served as a sandwich with cheese, home fries, ham, and eggs.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, boy. The waffle should be illegal. It actually is. You should have to weigh in before you can eat that thing. Nice job, guys. Thanks, man. Thank you very much. Appreciate it.

restaurants.name: Huerto
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in Portland, Oregon. Love this town. Great food town. Got my nieces up here, my buddy Pete. Come up here all the time. But you know what? I've never been up here for Mexican food. So I hear about this place. I'm ready to check it out. And then I find out all they have on the menu is tortas. You know, Mexican sandwiches? And that's the least likely thing I'd order at a Mexican restaurant. And everybody says, listen, don't judge the book by the cover, right, Fieri? Exactly. This is Huero, number one tortas. But this is actually Mexican 2.0 from Megan Sanchez and Alec Morrison.

dishes.description: Huaraches (mashed potatoes, masa, salt, oil, water, chili, jalapeno, par-baked, pan-fried). Roasted tamarind tomatoes (tomatoes, rosemary, avocado leaf, habanero, garlic, diluted tamarind paste, oil, salt, pepper, roasted). Jalapeno mayo (charred chilies, garlic, salt, lime juice, mayo). Assembled on huaraches with jalapeno mayo, tamarind tomatoes, queso blanco, fresh onion with cilantro and lime, avocado slices, shredded cabbage, and pickled onion.
dishes.guy_response: That might be one of the best vegetarian Mexican tortas I've ever had. Great crunch. It's creamy. It's spicy. It's tangy. It's rich. The acidity of the pickled onion is a must. The avocado flies right in. Then you get a little bit of fat coming through from that cheese. That's dynamite. Anyway, you saw it. It's delicious.

restaurants.name: Gabino's Creperie
restaurants.guy_intro: And that passion for creative crepes was cooked into Marcel Ramirez as a kid. I remember being on my dad's lap. Just whipping up batter. And it ended up changing his life. I was up to no good. And I said, I got to do things a little different here. So I just kind of started experimenting and created a non-traditional savory crepe. Don't tell the French that they got nothing on these guys. But good luck finding it your first time. If you drive by, there's a chance you won't even see it. I would describe it as a hole in the wall. Tucked away. Super cute in a little alley. Then the little partial door window over here is where they serve the food out.

dishes.description: Crepe filled with thinly sliced ribeye, cooked with oil, salt, pepper, garlic, and grilled onions. Topped with cheese sauce (processed cheese, whole milk, salt), chimichurri, and Mama G sauce.
dishes.guy_response: Everything you love about cheese steak. Tender beef. Caramelized onions. Processed cheese on there. A little extra creaminess coming from the Mama G sauce. What went through those? I thought it was cheese. The top is where you typically get that big cheese steak bun. Don't get me wrong. I love it. Mm-hmm. But not all the time does everybody want all that. This supports the experience. Not nearly as much carb, and it gives you a whole nother level of texture. Outstanding. Thank you. Delicious.

restaurants.name: Daybird
restaurants.guy_intro: Nashville hot fried chicken, but in a Sichuan Chinese ingredient style. You got the different levels of spice. You got the different dipping sauces. How do you explain? I mean, there's just a little chicken neon on the outside. They're only open for lunch. That's how you explain it. Look what a chicken. Hey, man, you got to see a chicken in a strip mall. But when you're a proven winner, you got license to do things your own way. Tonight is a very special Diners, Drivers & Dives. The home of a Tournament of Champions champ. From season four, the one and only champ, Chef Mei Lin. And she wears the belt.

dishes.description: Chicken thighs brined for 12 hours. Chili oil (Szechuan peppercorns, black peppercorns, coriander, fennel, cumin, star anise, bay leaves, cinnamon, chili de arbol, gochugaru chilies, oil, sugar). Pickling liquid and pickled vegetables (shallots, Fresnos, jalapenos). Hot honey. No heat spice mixture. Chicken dredged and double-fried. Habanero ranch and Daybird sauce. Served on a bun with hot honey, slaw, pickled shallots, and pickled chilies.
dishes.guy_response: The habanero? The habanero. Wow, that's so crunchy. One of the best chicken crunches I've had. It's very juicy. I feel like I have to open my mouth real big for this one. The full sandwich bite with the slaw, that's the kicker. Slaw is money. All the pickled veg in there is really dynamite. Thank you. I love that little extra honey kiss, but definitely the habanero ranch is my go-to. Amazing ranch. How's the extreme treating you? He's a little redder than he was when we got here. Like, I just took a tiny bite of a tiny edge of your chicken and I'm like, I can't even feel my tongue anymore. It's a really dynamite sandwich. Thank you.

restaurants.name: Grand Ole BBQ y Asado
restaurants.guy_intro: All right, now, San Diego is right here in the extra-like terrestrial solar system. This is way out in the middle of nowhere. San Diego was starving for barbecue. It had absolutely nothing. It was the moon of barbecue. The moon of barbecue? Troy, it's a half-hour show. You're just gonna like it, man. This is Grand Ole Barbecue. It has good southern barbecue, which you don't get in San Diego often. It's Central Texas barbecue, to be exact. And it's in owner and San Diego boy Andy Harris' blood. My dad's from Texas. Spent a lot of summers in Central Texas area eating good barbecue. So I got a smoker, just trying to duplicate those flavors. I wanted to bring Texas to San Diego. Eventually teaming up with Chef Ami Cisneros on a new kind of jam.

dishes.description: Beef rib seasoned with butcher grind pepper and salt, then smoked for 4 hours with white oak. Served on polenta with chorizo queso and pickled onion relish.
dishes.guy_response: Great smoke. Nice bark. Boy, that sounds really delicious. Each bite gets better. I'm going to try it. I'm going to try it. I'm going to try it. I'm going to try it. I'm going to try it. It gets better. Really? What I did is I first just started with the barbecue. Now, I'm enjoying some of the queso and the relish. It's delicious. I like just... Mmm. It's fantastic. Mmm. The fresh relief that you're getting from this corn chip salsa is dynamite. All right. Let's get in there. This elevates the barbecue to another level. I've done a lot of barbecue joints. I've never seen anything like this. And that relish is totally San Diego, too. That Mexican flavor, those serranos in there, that's legit.

restaurants.name: Rosine's Restaurant
restaurants.guy_intro: Authentic Italian's been headlining this joint since 1980, when Chef Jim Kulkowski's parents, Jim and Rosine, made it a family affair. Everything is absolutely amazing. I've never had a bad meal here. How long you worked here? All my life, basically. Since 1980. Your mom's place? My mom's. Rosine. Really? Yeah. Is your mom Italian? Yes. If you got it right, you'll catch Rosine herself helping out in the kitchen. This is definitely a family. You couldn't get any more family than this. They're always walking around to make sure you got everything you want. You're going to get a wonderful home-cooked meal, a wonderful portion.

dishes.description: Beef (top brown) cooked with dry red beans, granulated onion, granulated garlic, sugar, oregano, sweet basil, black pepper, bay leaves, white pepper, tomato paste, beef base, water, and diced tomatoes. Cooked for 4 hours. Beef pulled and shredded. Celery, carrots, zucchini, onions, and cabbage added. Cooked for 1 hour. Served with pasta and Parmesan cheese.
dishes.guy_response: I love the cabbage in there, the shredded beef. Not something you typically find in minestrone. A nice, rich broth. I mean, the whole thing. Great. It's delicious. But I want to call it a real boss. Awesome. Rosene, are you back there? Now this is the authority. You got a lot to be proud of, girl. Very nice to meet you. Thank you, Guy. Very nice to meet you. And look at her. I mean, to the nines. The hair, the bling. We have a rule that there is no, you can't wear more bling than the host of the show. Oh my gosh.

restaurants.name: Dick's Northwest Sausage and Deli
restaurants.guy_intro: So you took over right then when your dad passed? And then you guys met? I was working at the brewery. The brewery did start here, and then now it's at a bigger facility. But from the menu that I saw, you scratch make just about everything. We make all the meat here, except for our turkey. You always know you're getting a fresh cut of meat on your sandwich. We come in for the beer and stay for the food.

dishes.description: Pastrami (top round, corned beef cure, pink curing salt, injected, tumbled for 5 hours, dry-rubbed with coriander, garlic powder, paprika, brown sugar, crack black pepper, smoked for 6 hours at 140-175 degrees with hickory, steamed for 4 hours). Lacquer (drippings, brown sugar, black pepper, coriander, reduced by half). Served on buttered rye bread with provolone, Swiss cheese, mayonnaise, mustard, and sauerkraut.
dishes.guy_response: Toast on the bread is money. The slice on the pastrami is a perfect tour. There's texture, but not so thin that it's just falling apart. Not judging. Provolone Swiss combo, great. And the right amount of sauerkraut. You do everything right, excellent sandwich. Well done. Now go find the sausages.

restaurants.name: The Refuge
restaurants.guy_intro: I know it's pretty evident, but there's particular types of food that I really dig, and pastrami's one of them. I'm talking about old school, real deal pastrami, the kind of pastrami you get on the East Coast when you go to Katz's Deli in the Lower East Side of Manhattan. So if you're on the West Coast, I hear you gotta go to San Carlos, California, right between San Francisco and San Jose, and check out this joint, The Refuge. Everybody is over the moon about the pastrami, here. The Reuben? It just really, literally melts in your mouth. What do you think of this? It's the best. Like you'd have a wedding cake made out of pastrami. Oh, yeah. Must be the ingredients or the love that they put into it that makes it fantastic. It's a little bit of both, because owner Matt Levin's been working to perfect his recipe since he was 16, and the pastrami old guard was no help. Deli guys are gonna kick you right out of there. I had to figure it out myself. So he brought in his own heavy hitter, Chef Michael Royal. Tell me he doesn't look like a man. He's a culinary assassin. To give old school pastrami a try on the West Coast.

dishes.description: Pastrami (brined for a week, seasoned with coriander and black pepper, smoked with hickory, steamed for 4 hours). Lacquer (drippings, brown sugar, black pepper, coriander, reduced by half). Served on toasted rye bread with Russian dressing, coleslaw, and Swiss cheese.
dishes.guy_response: You can be a hard-core New Yorker. Nothing's gonna prepare you for that. It's that good, man. Really nice toasted rye. Perfectly brined, smoked, steamed. It's that good. I'm not kidding you. Night-night.

restaurants.name: Joan's Comfort Food Cafe
restaurants.guy_intro: They is Joan Sampson, who started in the biz at age 10, running a snack shack out of her bed. It's her parents' garage. Then college took her down a different path. I went to Rutgers University. What did you go to Rutgers for? For law. You're an attorney? I have a law degree. But instead of taking the bar, she opened up some restaurants, including this spot in 07. Doesn't matter what you do, end up back in the restaurant. They just suck you back in.

dishes.description: Chuck roast seared in butter, then braised with mirepoix, rosemary, coltine, bay leaf, peppercorns, garlic, stock, and wine for 4-6 hours at 275 degrees. Served with polenta potatoes, caramelized onions, wrapped in an orange tortilla with gravy, and garnished with lettuce and parsley.
dishes.guy_response: The gravy is ridiculous. Creaminess of potato, texture of the polenta, the rich pot roast. It is. A power bomb of flavor. Warning, don't eat this and drive. This is a coma item.

restaurants.name: Seasons and Regions Seafood Grill
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in the southwest area of Portland, Oregon, to check out a place where the chef came from a long background in the corporate world. And finally, one day, he said he's going to break free. That's right, he's going to do things on his own terms, do things his own way. He's going to cook from his favorite seasons and his favorite regions. Wait a second, that'd be a perfect name for a restaurant. This is Seasons and Regions Seafood Grill.

dishes.dish_name: Northwest Seafood Cioppino with Dungeness Crab Cake
dishes.description: Cioppino broth (olive oil, carrots, onions, celery, garlic, thyme, fennel, oregano, bay leaf, kosher salt, sugar, red wine vinegar, red wine, lemon juice, basil puree, anchovy paste, plum stock, tomato paste, diced tomato, crushed tomato, water, cooked for 2 hours). Crab cake (pre-cooked onions, egg, roasted red pepper, parsley, Tabasco, Worcestershire sauce, Old Bay seasoning, Dijon, local Dungeness crab, bread crumb, panko, deep fried). Seafood (salmon, rockfish, salt spring mussels). Assembled with clarified butter, salmon, rockfish, mussels, white wine, lemon juice, cioppino base. Topped with crab cake and chives.
dishes.guy_response: That's really good. That broth is fantastic. Typically, when you have cioppino, you know, you like to have some bread or something to dip it into. And this crab cake on top is perfectly crunchy, which would be kind of like the crusty bread going into it. Works incredibly well. Thank you, Chef, for wrecking it, because this is what I'm going to expect now, is a little salty, crunchy crab right on top. Something I didn't see coming, but I definitely would eat all day for the rest of the week.

restaurants.name: Frank's Noodle House
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here on Northeast Broadway in Portland, Oregon, just a few minutes from downtown. Now, this is a pretty busy street. Matter of fact, my fraternity brother, Pete, that lives here, says he's been up and down the street a million times and never stopped to check this joint out. That's too bad, because I hear that they are doing the real deal off the hook noodles. This is Frank's Noodle House.

dishes.description: Handmade noodles (all-purpose flour, water, salt, soybean oil). Chicken breast marinated in egg white, salt, and dashi. Cooked with soybean oil, garlic, cabbage, pepper, green bell pepper, onion, green onion, Chinese pepper, red chili powder, and soy sauce.
dishes.guy_response: This is it, you guys. You just taste the nice, fresh vegetables and these handmade noodles and the simply marinated chicken. Light. Veggies are still crunchy. Is it right? Delicious. Yeah. Oh, OK. I don't know.

restaurants.name: La Texanita
restaurants.guy_intro: Here in our hometown of Santa Rosa, California, Hunter and I have been rolling to this place for years. Now, there's all kinds of great Mexican restaurants in this area, but for some reason, between the story, the people, and the great food, La Texanita has become one of our favorites. Your favorite thing on the menu? Carne asada tacos. We take them down. And this really is where I bring my family and friends. How is it, Mom? Fantastic. Postole. Every time I bite into it, it reminds me of Mexico. Because owner Alma Mendez grew up there. Alma, how long have I known you? Five years. We both get our hair done by the same guy. The huarache carnitas is coming. Where did you get all the recipes from? From my mom, my sister. We have our own way to make our food. And for Alma, it's still the only way. Gracias. Gracias por venir. You make everything from scratch? Everything from scratch. The sofas, the carne asada, the tortillas, you name it. The handmade tortillas, so good. Tortillas. It makes a great difference.

dishes.description: Handmade tortillas (masa, water). Carne asada cooked with chopped onions, then lime juice added. Served with onions, cilantro, salsa, radishes, lime, and jalapenos.
dishes.guy_response: So simple, but so good. Onions almost have their own sweetness to them. And the natural juices and the fat from the meat cooked everything. But I'm telling you something. There's just nothing that can beat the handmade tortilla. And cooking it the right way. Shake your booty. And shake your booty the right way. Great job.

restaurants.name: La Unica Birria
restaurants.guy_intro: We got a line on Venice Boulevard. Let's get this place moving. La Unica Birria is the best place you will find. Birria 100%. Did you ever think this is what you would do? No. It's crazy. Yeah.

dishes.dish_name: Queso Tacos de Chivo (Goat Birria Tacos)
dishes.description: Goat birria (goat, birria paste made from California chile, black peppercorns, allspice, clove, cinnamon, garlic, onion, water), cooked for 3-4 hours. Served in handmade tortillas dipped in fat, with Monterey Jack cheese, cilantro, onions, lime, and chile de arbol salsa. Consomme for dipping.
dishes.guy_response: Without question, one of the best birria tacos I've ever had. Thank you very much. It's not gamey. It's not too spicy. It's not too salty. It's in balance. And then you get a great mixture of the onion and the cilantro. You've got to come down here. And do that dunk. If I could figure out how to get in the consomme in a kiddie pool, that's kind of weird, huh?

restaurants.name: Emma Jean's Hollenberger Cafe
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here on the California side of Route 66, just outside of Victorville, California. It is so hot. There are tons of trucks, and I am hungry. So now it's time to play the where am I going to stop and eat game. So with my keen sense of awareness, I'm going to stop and eat where the truckers eat, Emma Jean's Hollenberger Cafe. Folks around here say it's a trucker's paradise. After 60 years on this stretch of Route 66, Emma Jean's is just like it was. A little bit of history to it. I got it. It's still the must stop. They knew you. Best day. For great home cooking, like the signature biscuits and sausage gravy.

dishes.description: Biscuits made with flour, baking powder, and milk. Sausage gravy made with browned sausage, salt, pepper, and milk.
dishes.guy_response: That's a real deal biscuit right there. That's tasty. I don't think I could eat more than six or 12. I'm going to squabble these.

restaurants.name: Nikki's Thai Kitchen
restaurants.guy_intro: Can I have the semi-sweet and sour? It's absolutely the best Thai food I've ever had. Garlic chicken delight, please. Hold on to your socks. You're not going to expect it. Really awesome. It is awesome. You can't wait to dig in, because it's just so interesting. These are my buddies, the Morrises in the houses. They tell me that they've got this place, that they want to take me to for dinner. Out comes the food. And I didn't say anything, because I was inhaling the soup. I think I had about five portions. Yeah, a little bit of everything. Don't tell them. The food is great, but I really do think it's the people here. Dennis and Nikki sell the whole place. The dinner show is on all the time. We're talking about local native Dennis Schertz and his Thai-born wife, Nikki, who decided to share the food they love with the town they love in 06. As opposed to going just Thai, you said this is an Asian experience, a real Asian experience. A true experience. Exciting food with flavors you don't have every day.

dishes.description: Omelette, pork broth, and tamarind sauce (tamarind paste, sugar, salt, paprika, cooked for 3 hours). Cooked in a hot wok with oil, sweet radish, garlic, pork, noodles, tamarind sauce, chili, broth, bean sprouts, and onions. Served with peanuts.
dishes.guy_response: Too spicy? For me? Yeah. Oh. The tamarind sauce. Yes. That's really what gets the whole party started. Little sweet there, crunchy peanut. You get a touch of the chili flake coming through. I love that the noodles are really al dente. It's authentic. You make it just like you make for your family? It's my family like this, and my mom cook like this. That's it. Cooking is fun. You're fun, Nikki. Huh? I tell you what, no deal.

restaurants.name: 59er Diner
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm right outside of Leavenworth, Washington, on Highway 2. And I look to my right, and I go, wait a second. I'm not in New Jersey. But look at that beautiful, sparkling diner. I got to check it out. This is the 59er Diner. And even though this old school spot has hit a few bumps in the road, owner Wildman Joe Cannata, keeps the joint rocking and rolling. It's classic diner food at its finest.

dishes.description: Mozzarella logs coated in flour, then egg wash (sugar, garlic powder, oregano, paprika, dry mustard, lemon pepper, black pepper, milk), then panko, then egg wash again, then panko again. Deep fried. Served with homemade marinara (olive oil, garlic, plum tomatoes, salt, sugar, oregano, Italian seasoning).
dishes.guy_response: Ooh, that's gooey. That's what I'm talking about. The fact that you can contain all the cheese inside of this without having the blowouts is outstanding, because that's one of the biggest challenges. You fry it just the right amount to where the cheese is melted, not too oily, and you've still got really nice crunch. That's a legit cheese stick.

restaurants.name: From Scratch Restaurant
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm just like you. I like to go on road trips with my buddies, check out funky joints, eat great food. So here I am in Carmel, California to check out a joint where I hear they're scratch-making everything, and the biscuits are the bomb. This is from scratch. Okay, Robert, no more driving. Time to come in and eat. Let's go. Aw, Dad. I thought you said I could drive. Cinnamon French toast in the window. Homey, delicious meals. They put a little love in it. And that love comes from the family tree, because when Steven Griebing and his wife Carolyn bought the joint in 95, he most certainly tapped into his roots. Where did you learn to cook? My mother and my grandmother. Okay, so it's old school then. Old school. Yeah. Old school and hyperdrive.

dishes.dish_name: Extreme Double Sausage, Biscuits, and Gravy
dishes.description: Cheese biscuits (cheddar) topped with a sausage patty infused with sambal, and creamy sausage gravy.
dishes.guy_response: The cheddar in the biscuit is bananas. Then on top of it, the sausage patty, but you know what? It's that sambal in that sausage patty that just gives it a little bit of that extra heat. Yep. The gravy's creamy. And it's not dry and gritty. It's thick and pretty. You have license to use Extreme, man. That's pretty out of bounds. I got to tell you, it's amazing. I don't eat fat, and this is really good. I mean, it's really good. Thank you. Nice job. Excellent job, Chef. Thank you, thank you.

dishes.description: Chili with large chunks of brisket, raisins, and a balanced blend of heat and sweetness.
dishes.guy_response: The meat's tender, but you find the meat. Right. And that's why you cube it large. You taste the little sweetness of the raisin in there. There's a counterbalance to that heat. I get a couple levels of sweetness. That's a phenomenal chili. Thank you. Just the right amount of heat. We're going to have to go to the gym after this, aren't we? Yeah.

restaurants.name: El Burrito Mercado
restaurants.guy_intro: It really has the sazon of the family. You really know the sazon, the flavor of the family. That would be the Munoz family, with veteran chef Papa Tito leading the charge. Have you always been this close of a family? All the time. We always did a lot of things together. Like taking the plunge and opening this joint in 2012 with a menu based on mom's recipes. This food is delicious, it's fresh, it's flavorful. You just can't go wrong with anything.

dishes.description: Pork butt cooked in water with salt, onions, Mexican oregano, garlic, and bay leaves. Sauce made with Mexican oregano, black pepper, onions, garlic, cumin, allspice, guajillo, and California chili de arbol. Masa (salt, baking soda, shortening, pork stock). Assembled in softened corn husks with pork and sauce, then steamed.
dishes.guy_response: They're delicious. The masa has great texture. The pork is delicious. Not too spicy. You got a good ratio. It's not a bunch of masa and a little bit of pork inside. This is like a nice 50-50. Just the right amount of sauce. Hot, but not too hot. It leaves a nice little bite in your mouth. Everything you order is gonna be good, so it's really fun to try different things.

restaurants.name: The Junkyard
restaurants.guy_intro: And that's how owners Craig and Kim Zumwalt like it. Big and bold. Working in Nashville's music biz, Craig headed back home and opened this place in 06. How many people live here in Lancaster? Probably about 30. Wow. Is this a trailer? It's a race trailer. And then you built a shed on next to it? Yes. And they're definitely trying to kick it into gear when it comes to their high-octane signature burger.

dishes.description: One-pound burger patty, concave-smashed with onion and bacon in the middle. Served on a buttered bun with Parmesan, with a hole in the middle filled with an egg, cheddar cheese sauce, and cheddar. Topped with a cheese skirt (Parmesan, cheddar, onion, matzo, bacon), lettuce, mayonnaise, tomato, and pickle.
dishes.guy_response: Perfect. Look at this. Look at that hummingbird bite I took out of it. So, well, I'm going to take the rest of the month to enjoy this. Here's the thing, buddy. I've seen some big blocks in the design and disaster category. You pretty much are taking first place on that. I got that going for me. Which is nice. Yeah. Way to go. Way to go.

dishes.description: Crustless pizza made on a grill with Parmesan, cheddar, onion, gorgonzola, pepperoni, and mozzarella. Topped with homemade pizza sauce, olive, grilled pineapple, and in-house pesto. Covered with a dome to melt cheese, then topped with sliced tomatoes.
dishes.guy_response: This right here is just a gigantic thing of that crunchy fried cheese that you love on the outside of a quesadilla. The fact that you put toppings on it, it's crazy. Fried cheese. Can't beat it. Hey, here, Kerry. Here's your fried cheese pizza. It's extremely addicting. You have to share it. Everybody wants to try it. I have never seen a fried cheese pizza anywhere else. Junkyard is unique. Foods that you just can't find anywhere else. This is a hidden gem.

restaurants.name: Catelli's
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here at a cool little town called Geyserville in the Northern California wine country to check out a place that I kind of have some history with. Imagine a coach feels really proud when one of his players turns pro. Because that's how I feel when one of my guys that's worked with me in my restaurants ventures out to open his own place. That brings us here to Catelli's. Here we go, folks. Get your food ready. Where Nick Catelli. And his sister, Dominica. Have come home. My nonny and nonno, Virginia and Santi, opened Catelli's in 1935. And Nick and Dominica figured. They'd be running it someday. Because we've always dreamt of it. But the family lost the business back in 91. Once that happened, my sister and I were very adamant. We will open this again. It will be the family business. And it is, since they took over a year ago. And started serving the old Catelli classics.

dishes.description: Ravioli filling (pork butt, chardonnay, black peppercorn, fennel seed, red pepper flakes, fresh thyme, garlic, fresh ground nutmeg, salt, beef chunks, chicken, house-made sausage, olive oil, Swiss chard, pecorino, parmesan, thyme, basil, Italian parsley, rosemary, sage, eggs). Dough (egg, topia zero flour). Served with butter, garlic, salt, parsley, and Parmesan cheese.
dishes.guy_response: So tender, delicate, and so rich. Small, dainty, handmade perfection is what this is. This is one of the best raviolis I've ever had.

restaurants.name: La Tostadaria
restaurants.guy_intro: Welcome back. I am hanging out at La Tostadaria with Chef Fernando. This guy's got a story that you will love. Comes from Mexico. Goes to Canada. Opens a taqueria. Comes down to the United States. Opens a carnitas joint here in Grand Central Market. Then goes to culinary school. Elevates himself in the world of food, and opens up La Tostadaria. Really fresh seafood, great ingredients, and they definitely take their time for every plate. It's a cross between a great ceviche joint and a taco stand and a tostada stand.

dishes.description: Wild Mexican shrimp, Oaxaca cheese bun, chipotle aioli, lettuce, tomato, avocado mango radish salsa (mango, red pepper, chives, cilantro, apple cider vinegar, salt, agave nectar, avocado), raw onion, and shredded potatoes for crunch.
dishes.guy_response: Good news is that's fantastic. The bad news is the crew's hitting the vending machine. When you bite into it, you get the saltiness of the cheese, the texture of the cheese, the shrimp's cooked perfectly, and then all the accoutrement, the shredded lettuce, the chipotle mayo, the tomato, the raw onion, the phenomenal salsa with the mango and the avocado. I pride myself in my burgers. I'm known for my burgers. That right there in my expectation level of burgers, that is about as high as they go. Thank you. Outstanding. Thank you.

restaurants.name: OB Noodle House
restaurants.guy_intro: All right, ho dad, this is the joint. How's the food? Food's amazing and the story's even better. That's my kind of joint. This is the OB Noodle House. Ocean Beach is a really small beach community. They're very proud of their identity. This restaurant definitely fits in. 40 plus taps of really good beer. And what goes with beer? Of course, wings.

dishes.description: Mid-thigh chicken wings marinated for a day in garlic, salt, pepper, sugar, and chili oil saute (hot oil, ground ginger, garlic, ground lemongrass, ground onions, chili paste, chili flake, habanero, Thai chili pepper, peanut butter, hoisin sauce). Deep fried, then quick sauteed in a wok with hot oil, garlic, pepper, seasoning (sugar, granulated garlic, white pepper), and green onions. Garnished with cilantro.
dishes.guy_response: Oh my gosh. Yeah, a little bit of heat, not too much. Good amount of spice to it, a little bit of sweet. Just a little bit of the crispiness on the skin. They're tender, they're juicy. There's a lot of flavor, a lot of depth of flavor. I'm impressed.

restaurants.name: Pyrenees Cafe
restaurants.guy_intro: Pyrenees is a landmark. The food here is incredible. Good flavors, made from scratch, right from home. And right from the Basque tradition. What's the book got? This Old World menu has been a longtime staple here. So when Julie and Rod Crawford took over in 2007, and 2014, they weren't about to change it. Basque food, is it hard to explain that to people in Bakersfield? No, everybody in Bakersfield gets it. It's hard to explain to the rest of the world. Traditionally, it is ethnic food that comes from the Basque region between France and Spain. And French-born chef Gilbert Hernandez knows a thing or two about the Basque people.

dishes.description: Chicken pieces seasoned with salt, black pepper, garlic powder, chicken base, fresh garlic, and olive oil. Marinated for at least two hours. Then dipped in butter and milk, dredged in seasoned flour (black pepper, salt). Pan-fried (Basque way). Served with green beans, French fries, and garlic sauce (fresh garlic, olive oil, butter, parsley).
dishes.guy_response: The garlic on the outside is very strong. But the garlic and the flavor inside the chicken is what is really nice. It's tender, it's juicy, lots of flavor. The crust on the chicken is great. I have restaurants called Johnny Garlic's. I have one in Bakersfield. But if I was going to ever make fried garlic chicken, that would be the example. Outstanding. The garlic just hits you. Right away, as soon as you bite into it. Probably don't want to have your first date after you eat it. But it's very, very good. The best chicken that you'll ever have. Even though it's rooted in humble beginnings. Basque food is peasant food. Cuts of meat nobody wanted. And you had to figure out a way to make those meats flavorful and tasteful. How close is this to traditional, original Basque like you get at home? Probably a little better than what we had at home. If I lived in this town, this would be a place that I would go to at least once a week. I mean, it feels like I'm in an old Basque restaurant, because I am. Mm-hmm. And the food matches. It's delicious. Wonderful job.
